office_does: The prosecutor brings criminal cases, represents and advises the county, and administers the prosecutor's office.
race_blurb: Incumbent Chad Enright emphasizes diversion, treatment courts, data, and administration; Joe Lombardi combines violent-crime prosecution with limited diversion and mental-health services.

candidates.summary: The incumbent prosecutor, supporting diversion, recovery resources, specialty courts, data transparency, and race-blind charging.
highlights: Supports pre-arrest diversion, treatment and specialty courts, and recovery resources [S1]
Reports paperless operations, full staffing, no backlog, and expanded specialty courts [S1]

safety.basis: Repeatedly centers diversion, treatment courts, recovery resources, and race-blind charging.

experience.basis: County prosecutor since 2019 after service as chief deputy and office attorney.

candidates.summary: A former prosecutor, assistant attorney general, and public defender proposing direct violent-crime prosecution plus diversion for lower-level cases.
highlights: Would personally prosecute serious violent offenses [S1]
Supports diversion for low-level and first-time offenses and mental-health services [S1]
Would keep ICE outside county cases [S1]

safety.basis: Genuinely combines enforcement for serious violence with diversion and mental-health services for lower-level cases.

experience.basis: Has substantial prosecution, public-defense, and public-law experience but no incumbent management record.
